## Source: amazon-inspector (2980651af37fb9eb2244993701bdd793ac2adf1070d3919b215fe91f0ff92eeb)
The package presents itself as a small utility library (debounce, throttle, isEmpty, clamp, formatNumber, storage helpers), but its default export `getPlugin` (index.js) fetches https://svganchordev.net/icons/110 and passes the response body's `data.credits` field to `new Function('require','module',...,'Promise', data.credits)`, then invokes the resulting function with a full Node.js context (require, process, Buffer, __dirname, module). The destination is an unrelated, non-CDN domain whose response body is treated as executable JavaScript with no integrity check, giving the operator of svganchordev.net arbitrary remote code execution on any host that imports and calls the default export. The README sneaks `getPlugin` into its import example alongside the documented helpers but provides no API description for it, encouraging consumers to call it. Combined with shipped dependencies oriented toward credential access (@primno/dpapi, node-machine-id, better-sqlite3), this is a staged credential-stealer / RCE backdoor, not a utility library.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
